description: Replace the resource-name-only document-reference failure surface in `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpsertResult.cs` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpdateResult.cs` with a path-aware per-occurrence contract for DMS-982. Acceptance: the new contract can represent every failing document-reference instance with its concrete JSON path, target resource, and an internal reason enum at minimum covering `Missing` and `IncompatibleTargetType`; the contract is usable by both POST and PUT handlers; and descriptor failure contracts remain available for existing 400-style mapping.
steps-to-verify: Add or update unit tests that construct the new result types and confirm every failing occurrence can be represented without collapsing to unique resource names.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Update `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpsertHandler.cs`, `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpdateByIdHandler.cs`, and any shared response helpers so path-aware document-reference failures become 409 responses keyed by concrete JSON path instances. Acceptance: existing status semantics stay unchanged, every failing path instance is emitted in `validationErrors`, duplicate referential ids at different JSON locations produce multiple entries, and descriptor failures continue to map to 400 validation responses.
steps-to-verify: Add handler tests covering POST and PUT document-reference failures with multiple concrete paths for the same missing referential id.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Extend the backend-to-Core descriptor failure contract and Core handler mapping so DMS-982 can distinguish `Missing` versus `DescriptorTypeMismatch` per failing descriptor path while preserving the current client-visible 400 status and overall validation-error shape. Acceptance: `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpsertResult.cs` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpdateResult.cs` carry enough per-path reason data for Core to render different messages; `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpsertHandler.cs` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpdateByIdHandler.cs` emit a different validation message for type mismatch than for missing descriptors; and no other client-exposed contract differences are introduced.
steps-to-verify: Add handler/result tests covering both missing-descriptor and descriptor-type-mismatch paths for POST and PUT, asserting the same 400/error shape with different messages.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Add the reusable relational write-prerequisite resolver service and contracts for DMS-982 without wiring a new relational `IDocumentStoreRepository` path. Acceptance: the service accepts request-scoped extracted references plus `MappingSet`/resource context, performs per-request memoization and dedupe, preserves per-occurrence diagnostics in a `ResolvedReferenceSet`, and exposes a narrow adapter seam so future auth/write prerequisite batching can append SQL without redesigning the service contract.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for request-scoped dedupe and memoization behavior using fake adapter results.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Define the shared request-scoped relational access contract used by the DMS-982 resolver and dialect adapters. Acceptance: shared resolver/adapter code depends on a narrow abstraction for command execution and result reading rather than repository operations, the contract is small enough to support future batched write-prerequisite composition without redesign, and contract-focused unit tests validate fake/in-memory implementations used by resolver tests.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering the shared relational access abstraction and fake/test implementations used by the resolver.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Implement the PostgreSQL request-scoped relational access path behind the shared DMS-982 contract. Acceptance: PostgreSQL resolver commands execute against the current request's data source/connection path, the implementation reuses existing request-scoped PostgreSQL infrastructure instead of introducing repository wiring, and focused unit tests cover connection selection and command execution behavior needed by the resolver.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for PostgreSQL relational access behavior through the shared contract.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Implement the SQL Server request-scoped relational access path behind the shared DMS-982 contract. Acceptance: SQL Server resolver commands execute from the active request-scoped connection-string path without introducing full repository wiring, the implementation exposes the same shared contract used by PostgreSQL, and focused unit tests cover connection creation and command execution behavior needed by the resolver.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for SQL Server relational access behavior through the shared contract.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Build the request-scoped resolved reference surface that downstream write execution will consume. Acceptance: given extracted reference occurrences plus deduped resolver results, runtime can materialize a deterministic successful-resolution view keyed by extracted reference occurrence/path; duplicate referential ids at different JSON locations remain separate occurrences in the surface; and unresolved or incompatible occurrences are excluded from successful resolutions while still remaining available in failure diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering repeated referential ids across multiple concrete JSON paths and ensuring only successful resolutions populate the successful-resolution surface.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Implement metadata helpers that derive allowed target `ResourceKeyId` sets for document-reference validation from the runtime mapping set and derived relational model. Acceptance: concrete targets resolve to exactly one allowed `ResourceKeyId`, abstract/superclass targets resolve to the allowed concrete member keys for the hierarchy, descriptor resources are excluded from document-reference compatibility checks, and missing mapping-set metadata fails fast with deterministic errors.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering concrete target, abstract/superclass target, descriptor exclusion, and fail-fast missing-metadata cases.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Implement the PostgreSQL bulk lookup SQL shape and parameterization for DMS-982. Acceptance: document and descriptor referential ids can be emitted into a set-based PostgreSQL lookup against `dms.ReferentialIdentity`, no per-reference query loop is introduced, the SQL shape can later participate in a larger batched write-prerequisite command, and focused unit tests cover empty, small, and large deduped lookup sets plus deterministic parameter ordering.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for PostgreSQL SQL shape generation and parameter binding across representative lookup sizes.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Implement PostgreSQL adapter execution and raw row projection over the shared resolver contract. Acceptance: the adapter executes the PostgreSQL lookup through the shared relational access seam, projects rows from `dms.ReferentialIdentity`, `dms.Document`, and `dms.Descriptor` into shared raw result records, returns enough membership/type data for later shared failure classification, and preserves deduped lookup semantics while retaining per-occurrence inputs for diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for PostgreSQL raw result projection covering found, missing, alias-row, and descriptor-membership row shapes.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Implement the SQL Server small-list lookup strategy for DMS-982 using a simple parameterized approach beneath safe thresholds. Acceptance: small deduped lookup sets execute without risking the 2,100-parameter limit, document and descriptor lookups share one resolver-oriented raw row contract, no per-reference query loop is introduced, and focused unit tests cover deterministic parameterization and row projection for representative small inputs.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for the SQL Server small-list strategy and raw row projection contract.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Implement the SQL Server threshold-safe bulk lookup strategy for large DMS-982 reference sets. Acceptance: large deduped lookup sets switch to a bulk-safe strategy before risking the 2,100-parameter limit, the strategy returns the same raw adapter row contract as the small-list path, and focused unit tests cover threshold crossing and deterministic batching behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests that exercise the SQL Server threshold-crossing path and bulk-safe lookup mechanics.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Implement SQL Server adapter orchestration that selects between the small-list and bulk strategies and exposes the shared raw resolver result contract. Acceptance: strategy selection is deterministic, the adapter returns the membership/type data required for later shared failure classification, and focused unit tests assert raw-result parity with PostgreSQL across found, missing, incompatible-target, and descriptor-membership row shapes.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit tests for SQL Server strategy selection and raw-result parity with PostgreSQL.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Implement shared failure classification over adapter results for both document and descriptor references. Acceptance: unresolved document references and wrong-type document matches surface through the new path-aware document-reference contract with internal reasons kept distinct for tests/logging, descriptor failures distinguish at least `Missing` versus `DescriptorTypeMismatch`, and every failing concrete JSON path instance is preserved even when the underlying lookup key was deduped.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that exercise mixed document/descriptor failures and repeated failing paths sharing the same deduped key.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Define the shared resolver composition surface without yet wiring repository request paths. Acceptance: the resolver, relational access seam, and dialect adapter factory/contracts are registered behind a narrow interface that both production PostgreSQL wiring and SQL Server test wiring can consume; the surface does not require `IDocumentStoreRepository` integration; and unit tests can build a service collection around the new shared contract without pulling in full frontend startup.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that build a minimal service collection around the shared resolver composition surface and adapter factory.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Wire the shared DMS-982 composition surface into the PostgreSQL production registration path. Acceptance: when PostgreSQL datastore services are registered with `UseRelationalBackend` enabled, the prerequisite resolver, relational access seam, and PostgreSQL adapter factory can all be resolved from the application service collection without introducing a new relational repository execution path.
steps-to-verify: Add or update DI tests covering PostgreSQL service registration through the current frontend/bootstrap path.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Provide SQL Server registration/test wiring for the shared DMS-982 composition surface. Acceptance: SQL Server-focused tests can construct the resolver, relational access seam, and SQL Server adapter factory through the same shared contract/factory seam used by PostgreSQL, without requiring new frontend datastore bootstrap or `IDocumentStoreRepository` request-path integration in this story.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that resolve the SQL Server adapter factory and resolver from a service collection using the shared composition surface.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Add shared resolver fixture models and seed helpers for DMS-982 that both dialect-specific integration harnesses can consume. Acceptance: shared helpers describe compact resolver datasets for `dms.ResourceKey`, `dms.Document`, `dms.ReferentialIdentity`, `dms.Descriptor`, and any required abstract alias/member rows; the helpers do not own dialect-specific schema provisioning or reset; and they can express both successful and fail-closed compatibility scenarios needed by later integration tasks.
steps-to-verify: Add shared fixture/seeding helpers reused by both PostgreSQL and SQL Server resolver integration tests.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj`

Acceptance: the PostgreSQL integration project can create the minimal resolver schema objects required by the shared fixtures, seed repeatable test datasets, and reset state between tests without relying on the full DMS E2E stack.
steps-to-verify: Add PostgreSQL integration fixture setup that provisions the minimal resolver test database shape used by the shared fixtures.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj`

Acceptance: the SQL Server integration project can create the minimal resolver schema objects required by the shared fixtures, seed repeatable test datasets, and reset state between tests without relying on the full DMS E2E stack.
steps-to-verify: Add SQL Server integration fixture setup that provisions the minimal resolver test database shape used by the shared fixtures.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj`

description: Add focused SQL Server integration coverage for document-reference resolution behavior. Acceptance: tests cover request-local dedupe versus per-path diagnostics, missing document references at multiple concrete JSON locations, concrete-target mismatch, and abstract/superclass alias resolution with fail-closed compatibility validation at the same public-result level as PostgreSQL.

description: Add focused SQL Server integration coverage for descriptor validation behavior. Acceptance: tests cover descriptor membership failure when a resolved document is not present in `dms.Descriptor`, resolved wrong-descriptor-type failure when a descriptor row exists but the resolved resource type does not match the expected descriptor resource, missing-lookup wrong-type failure when the normalized URI implies a different descriptor type than the expected resource, and parity of `ResolvedReferenceSet.InvalidDescriptorReferences` outcomes with PostgreSQL and the shared classifier contract despite the different adapter strategy under the hood.

description: Add focused SQL Server integration coverage for threshold-sensitive bulk lookup behavior. Acceptance: tests cover at least one threshold-crossing large deduped lookup set that exercises the SQL Server bulk-safe strategy and confirm repeated per-path diagnostics remain intact after dedupe.
steps-to-verify: Ensure the SQL Server admin connection string is configured for `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration`.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj`

description: Replace the duplicated descriptor-type mismatch heuristic in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/ReferenceResolver.cs` and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Old.Postgresql/ReferenceHelper.cs` with a single authoritative classification path. Acceptance: runtime no longer parses descriptor URI text to infer `DescriptorTypeMismatch` on a lookup miss; `DescriptorTypeMismatch` is emitted only when backed by authoritative resolved metadata or one documented shared helper with identical semantics across both code paths; genuinely missing or unknown descriptors remain `Missing`; and malformed or non-standard descriptor URIs do not produce divergent behavior between the relational resolver and the legacy PostgreSQL path.
steps-to-verify: Add or update unit tests covering: a missing descriptor whose URI text matches the expected descriptor type, a missing descriptor whose URI text implies a different descriptor type, a resolved descriptor row whose resource type does not match the expected descriptor resource, and a malformed or non-standard descriptor URI.
Confirm both the relational resolver and the legacy PostgreSQL reference helper use the same classification implementation or helper rather than duplicating URI parsing logic.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Extend the DMS-982 relational resolver lookup contract in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/ReferenceResolverContracts.cs` and the dialect-specific SQL projections so a resolved hit can be verified against the requested natural key instead of trusting `dms.ReferentialIdentity` alone. Acceptance: the request-side contract carries enough target identity context to re-check a hit, the result-side contract carries an authoritative verification witness for the matched document or descriptor identity, PostgreSQL and SQL Server adapters project that witness in bulk without per-reference queries, and same-type cross-wired rows can be distinguished from ordinary `Missing` and `IncompatibleTargetType` outcomes.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering the expanded request/result contracts and adapter row readers so the verification witness is preserved for both document and descriptor lookups.
Add SQL-shape tests for PostgreSQL and SQL Server confirming the new identity or URI projection is included in the bulk lookup command text and result mapping.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/ReferenceResolver.cs` and any shared helpers so the resolver re-validates every successful lookup against the requested natural key and fails closed on a corrupted `dms.ReferentialIdentity` row, including same-type cross-wiring. Acceptance: a lookup that resolves to the wrong existing document of the same resource type is never classified as a successful reference; the resolver raises a deterministic corruption or incident-grade failure instead of downgrading the case to `Missing`, `IncompatibleTargetType`, or `DescriptorTypeMismatch`; descriptor validation also verifies the resolved descriptor identity or URI matches the requested descriptor natural key; and the current same-request duplicate sanity check remains a secondary defense rather than the only corruption guard.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for document and descriptor references where the adapter returns a same-type but wrong-document hit, asserting the resolver fails closed with the dedicated corruption path.

Add or update integration tests on both PostgreSQL and SQL Server with deliberate `dms.ReferentialIdentity` corruption, asserting POST or PUT prerequisite resolution fails loudly and does not return a successful `ResolvedReferenceSet` entry for the cross-wired reference.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj`
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj`

description: Replace the mutually exclusive reference-failure result types in `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpsertResult.cs` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpdateResult.cs` with a single write-result contract that can carry both invalid document references and invalid descriptor references at the same time. Acceptance: POST and PUT result contracts can represent document-only, descriptor-only, and mixed-failure outcomes without dropping either side; the contract preserves per-path failure detail for both families; repository callers do not need to invent a precedence rule between `*FailureReference` and `*FailureDescriptorReference`; and contract-focused unit tests cover mixed outcomes explicitly.
steps-to-verify: Add or update unit tests constructing the new Upsert and Update failure result types for document-only, descriptor-only, and mixed-reference-validation failures.

Confirm no new repository integration point is forced to choose one failure family when both `ResolvedReferenceSet.InvalidDocumentReferences` and `ResolvedReferenceSet.InvalidDescriptorReferences` are populated.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Update `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpsertHandler.cs`, `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpdateByIdHandler.cs`, `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Utilities/ValidationErrorFactory.cs`, and any shared response helpers so a unified backend reference-validation failure is surfaced consistently to clients. Acceptance: document and descriptor validation errors are merged into one `validationErrors` payload keyed by concrete JSON path; repeated failures at different paths remain separate entries; POST and PUT handlers use one documented status-code rule for mixed failures instead of an implicit precedence rule; and pure document-only and descriptor-only behaviors remain backward compatible except where explicitly changed by the documented mixed-failure policy.
steps-to-verify: Add handler tests for POST and PUT mixed failures asserting the final response includes both document-reference and descriptor-reference validation messages in one payload.
Add unit tests for the shared validation-error builder covering mixed path collisions, document-only inputs, descriptor-only inputs, and merged mixed inputs.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Harden `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/ReferenceResolver.cs` so request-scoped memoization does not poison the resolver after an adapter failure or cancellation. Acceptance: if `IReferenceResolverAdapter.ResolveAsync(...)` throws or is cancelled before results are cached, a later `ResolveAsync(...)` call on the same `ReferenceResolver` instance can retry the same referential ids successfully; lookup metadata is only retained when a corresponding snapshot is safe to reuse or the failure path explicitly rolls it back; same-request compatibility checks still work across repeated calls after a successful lookup; and no retry path can fall through to `GetRequiredLookupSnapshot(...)` for an id that was previously requested but never successfully cached.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that use a fake adapter which throws on the first call and succeeds on the second call against the same `ReferenceResolver` instance, asserting the second call performs a real retry and succeeds.
Add unit tests covering adapter cancellation or failure cleanup plus normal successful miss memoization so misses are only reused after a completed lookup round.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Restore the documented mixed descriptor-plus-document reference failure behavior in `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpsertHandler.cs` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler/UpdateByIdHandler.cs`. Acceptance: when one backend write failure contains both invalid document references and invalid descriptor references, POST and PUT return the established 400 bad-request response shape instead of `409 unresolved-reference`; the merged `validationErrors` payload still includes both document and descriptor paths; document-only failures remain `409`; descriptor-only failures remain `400`; and the handler/unit-test expectations align with the existing `DescriptorReferenceValidation` mixed-failure contract.
steps-to-verify: Update or add handler tests for POST and PUT mixed-failure results asserting status `400`, the bad-request response body shape, and merged validation messages from both failure families.

If the local E2E stack is available, run the mixed descriptor-plus-document reference scenario from `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/References/DescriptorReferenceValidation.feature` and confirm it still returns `400`.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Align SQL-side reference verification identity serialization with Core's canonical datetime identity format across `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql/PostgresqlReferenceLookupCommandBuilder.cs`, `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql/MssqlReferenceLookupSmallListStrategy.cs`, and any shared helpers they depend on. Acceptance: verification keys for `ScalarKind.DateTime` use the same UTC `yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ` shape that Core emits after request normalization; successful lookups whose identity includes a datetime component do not false-fail as corruption; existing date-only, time-only, and non-datetime identity behavior remains unchanged; and new tests cover datetime identity serialization for both dialects.
steps-to-verify: Add backend unit tests covering PostgreSQL and SQL Server verification-key SQL generation or row projection for a reference identity that includes a `ScalarKind.DateTime` element.
Add at least one resolver-level test that exercises a datetime identity component and confirms a correctly matched lookup does not raise a corruption exception.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Remove or consolidate dead duplicate builders in `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Utilities/ValidationErrorFactory.cs`. Acceptance: either `BuildInvalidReferenceValidationErrors(...)` and `BuildInvalidDescriptorValidationErrors(...)` are removed because the merged write-path builder is the sole supported implementation, or they are retained only if real callers and tests justify them; there is one authoritative path for building write reference `validationErrors`; and handler-visible response content remains unchanged after the cleanup.
steps-to-verify: Delete or consolidate the duplicate helper methods and update any affected tests so there is no unreferenced duplicate error-builder path left behind.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Fix the ordering bug in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/ReferenceResolver.cs` so lookup-integrity verification runs for every non-null resolved hit before document or descriptor type classification. Acceptance: a resolved row whose verification witness does not match the requested natural key is always treated as corruption, even when the resolved `ResourceKeyId` would otherwise produce `IncompatibleTargetType` or `DescriptorTypeMismatch`; `Missing` remains the only classification path that bypasses integrity checks; document and descriptor corruption cases fail through the dedicated corruption path instead of being downgraded to client validation failures; and new tests cover wrong-key concrete-target and wrong-key descriptor-target scenarios explicitly.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests showing a non-null lookup with a mismatched verification key throws the corruption exception before document or descriptor failure classification can return `IncompatibleTargetType` or `DescriptorTypeMismatch`.
Add or update integration tests on PostgreSQL and SQL Server with deliberately cross-wired `dms.ReferentialIdentity` data that would previously have been misclassified as a validation error.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj`
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj`

description: Remove or justify the now-unused `GetResourceNames()` helpers from `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpsertResult.cs` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/UpdateResult.cs`. Acceptance: either the helpers are deleted because live handler mapping no longer consumes resource-name-only reference failures, or a current non-legacy caller is documented and tested; no production code retains redundant resource-name projection APIs that can drift from the path-aware failure contract; and any test coverage is updated to validate the remaining authoritative contract rather than preserving dead compatibility surface by default.
steps-to-verify: Search the non-legacy codebase for production callers of `GetResourceNames()` and remove the helpers if no current callers remain outside legacy-only tests.
Update or delete unit tests that exist solely to preserve the dead helper surface, keeping coverage focused on the path-aware failure contract and current handler behavior.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`
